summary: Machine policy for the first sign-in animation and Microsoft account service opt-in prompt. The local Microsoft Logon.admx file documents the same Policies\System\EnableFirstLogonAnimation value used by the app.

setting.name: First Sign-In Animation
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can show a first sign-in animation and, for Microsoft-account users, an opt-in prompt for services the first time someone signs in. This policy decides whether that first-run experience is shown.
setting.why_people_change_it: People turn it off on managed or repeatedly deployed systems because it speeds up the first sign-in experience and removes an extra onboarding step.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling it shortens first sign-in, but it also removes the normal animation and the Microsoft account service opt-in prompt.
setting.professional_notes: Logon.admx maps the machine policy to HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\EnableFirstLogonAnimation with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. The local ADML help text documents the special not-configured behavior for the first user versus later users.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows uses its built-in first-run behavior instead of forcing the animation on or off by policy.

allowed_values.label: Show the first sign-in animation
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is enabled, so users see the first sign-in animation and Microsoft-account users see the services opt-in prompt.

allowed_values.label: Skip the first sign-in animation
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled, so users do not see the animation and Microsoft-account users do not see the services opt-in prompt.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes EnableFirstLogonAnimation = 0, which matches the documented disabled-policy behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows machine-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows client devices with first sign-in animation support

states.rationale: The machine policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: When the policy is not configured, the user who completes initial Windows setup sees the animation on first sign-in, while users added later may not see it after setup has already been completed.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Devices that should keep the normal first-run onboarding flow

states.rationale: Leave first-run behavior under Windows control.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the normal onboarding experience on first sign-in.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the shortest possible first sign-in flow.
tradeoffs: The normal Windows first-run animation behavior is preserved.
Microsoft-account users may still see the services opt-in prompt.

recommended_profiles.label: Fast first sign-in
avoid_for: Users who want the full standard first-run experience

states.rationale: Disable the animation and related first-run prompt through policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want new or reset devices to reach the desktop faster.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to preserve the default onboarding visuals and prompt flow.
tradeoffs: The animation and Microsoft-account services opt-in prompt are skipped.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft Logon.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps the machine policy to HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\EnableFirstLogonAnimation with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft Logon.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text explains the enabled, disabled, and not-configured behavior for the first sign-in animation and the Microsoft-account services opt-in prompt.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes EnableFirstLogonAnimation = 0 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: The local Microsoft Logon.admx file explicitly defines EnableFirstLogonAnimation under Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0, and the app writes the documented disabled value.

validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file names the same policy key and EnableFirstLogonAnimation value used by the app. The paired ADML help text documents the first sign-in animation and Microsoft-account opt-in prompt behavior.
